data.title: CoreWeave becomes first hyperscaler to deploy Nvidia's GB300 NVL72
data.description:
CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV) has become the first hyperscaler to deploy Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) GB300 NVL72 liquid-cooled, rack-scale AI system, the company announced today.

CoreWeave shares surged 6% by noon trading on Thursday. Nvidia had edged up 1.4%. Dell Technologies (NYSE:DELL), another partner involved in the system, was up 1.7%.

The GB300 NVL72 features 72 Nvidia Blackwell Ultra GPUs and 36 Arm-based (ARM) Nvidia Grace CPUs. The unit is housed within Dell's integrated rack scale system. The system is 10 times faster in user responsiveness and has a five-time improvement in throughput per watt over the prior generation of Nvidia's Hopper.

It is built for frontier-scale AI and allows users to train, optimize and deploy multi-trillion parameter models, CoreWeave said. It's also engineered to handle test-time scaling inference, which is crucial for deploying advanced AI models.

Last month, Supermicro (SMCI) unveiled a range of enterprise AI solutions built on Nvidia's Blackwell architecture to accelerate AI factory deployments in the European market. The company introduced more than 30 solutions designed for air or liquid-cooled NVIDIA HGX B200, liquid-cooled NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 and NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition.

Core Scientific (CORZ), a long-time partner of CoreWeave, inched down about 1%. Last month, reports surfaced that CoreWeave was considering acquiring the company, prompting a stock surge. 

CoreWeave launched its initial public offering in late March 2025. Its stock value has quadrupled since its IPO date.

data.title: BofA ups PT for Roku on Amazon partnership, improving advertiser sentiment
data.description:
Analysts at Bank of America hiked their price target on Roku (NASDAQ:ROKU) and reiterated their bullish rating, citing improving macroeconomic sentiment and viewing its recently announced Amazon (AMZN) partnership as favorable.

The research firm said the uncertainty seen in the last few months for advertising trends "appears to be abating," and "sentiment has improved" as the second quarter progressed. 

They see the Amazon partnership "as another proof point that Roku intends to be more flexible and drive further interoperability within their platform."

The partnership will provide a significant opportunity as monetization of their advertising inventory improves going forward and further deepens their relationships with other DSPs, BofA said.

"Roku has notable scope to keep expanding its top and bottom line and should benefit from several favorable industry trends along with company-specific actions," BofA said in its July 3 research note. "These include 1) growth in CTV advertising, 2) secular growth in streaming video domestically and internationally, and 3) improving fill rates (the % of ad spaces being successfully filled) in its advertising business."

BofA raised its 2025 revenue forecast for Roku to $4.59B, from $4.55B. It also upped its FY EBITDA forecast to $360M from a prior guide of $351M.

ROKU has a PT of $110, up from $100, implying an upside of 27.3%. Stock is up 2.2% by midday on Thursday.

data.title: SA Asks: What's next for Ford, General Motors' EV businesses?
data.description:
What's next for Ford (NYSE:F) and General Motors' (NYSE:GM) EV businesses as they battle increasing competition and shifting macro headwinds?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Luca Socci and Envision Research for their thoughts.

Luca Socci: Steel tariffs, access to rare-earth minerals, and cooling consumer enthusiasm for still-expensive EVs have caused unexpected setbacks in the strategies Ford (NYSE:F) and GM (NYSE:GM) had designed to become market-leading players in this space after pouring billions into the transformation process. Recently, we learned that in an expanding U.S. car market, EV sales are still declining, threatening the returns on past investments many automakers were expecting to see from 2025 onward. 

In addition to general change in the geopolitical and economic landscape, Ford (NYSE:F) also kept facing quality control issues that even paused F-150 Lightning production for a couple of months.  Once solved, Ford (F) had to halt Mustang Mach-E sales due to a door-locking issue caused by a discharge of the battery. General Motors (NYSE:GM), on the other hand, has just recently achieved EV profitability, but now pushes together with other legacy automakers to overturn gas-powered cars banning regulations. 

While rare earth access seems to have been solved (for now), and higher input costs due to steel tariffs could be a temporary issue as the U.S. strengthens its domestic output, the real challenge comes from rethinking the model lineup for the next three to five years. To this extent, it seems an EV-only world won't appear soon. Rather, recent developments suggest we are going in the direction of an automotive space presenting multiple powering solutions. In particular, the trend I consider the right compromise between EVs and ICE is developing PHEV solutions, which seem to meet the consumers' favor both in terms of pricing and versatility.

I think both Ford and GM now understand that investing big in only one trend can backfire since trends, by nature, can suddenly change due to preference shifts or political turns.  We will see GM (GM) and Ford (F) moving more cautiously in an endeavor that could make many shareholders happy when they see that capex is more under control.

Envision Research: I am not optimistic about their EV prospects judging by the updates provided in their recent earnings report for fiscal Q1 2025. More specifically, in the earnings report, GM (GM) stopped providing a target for improvement in EV profitability. For me, I expect the volumes to be below its prior target of around 300K. Ford (F), similarly, also suspended forward guidance in its fiscal Q1 earnings report. 

The root causes for the above uncertainties are similar in my mind, and include ongoing supply chain disruptions, risks related to tariff charges, potential retaliatory actions from U.S. trading partners, and policy uncertainty related to emissions regulation. Looking past these immediate issues, I consider China’s auto-manufacturing ecosystem to be fundamentally more self-sufficient than the U.S.'s.  As such, I expect GM (GM) and Ford (F) to have trouble competing with Chinese automobile manufacturers like BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY) in the long term. 

  • Top Automobile Manufacturers Stocks

data.title: Regencell stock rally returns as company blames short selling for volatility
data.description:
Regencell Bioscience (NASDAQ:RGC) ended a 10-day losing streak on Thursday to climb ~120%, extending its YTD jump to over 180-fold, days after the Chinese biotech blamed short sellers, among other factors, for its extreme share price volatility.

With nearly 94% of its outstanding shares held by insiders, the Hong Kong-based Regencell initiated a 38-for-1 stock split last month to improve liquidity.

Despite the low float, the company attributed the volatility to a market phenomenon known as a short squeeze, where short sellers, in the event of a sharp rise in the price of their shorted stock, are forced to buy the underlying shares to cover their positions, fueling a further spike in the asset’s price.

“We believe that a “short squeeze” due to a sudden increase in demand for our ordinary shares that largely exceeds supply has led to, and may continue to lead to, extreme price volatility in shares of our ordinary shares,” Regencell (NASDAQ:RGC) said with its interim results for H2 2024 on Monday.

According to market data, only about 3% of Regencell’s (NASDAQ:RGC) float indicates short interest, which, according to Ihor Dusaniwsky, managing director of data analytics firm S3 Partners, means that a short squeeze is unlikely to be a reason for the stock’s volatility.

“Looking at the size of the short selling compared to the trading volume, it can’t be the cause,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Dusaniwsky as saying during an interview.

“Long buying and long selling was by far the primary driver of its price moves,” Dusaniwsky noted, adding that the number of shorts “are a drop in the bucket compared to total average trading volume.”

data.title: Mortgage rates see biggest weekly decline since early March
data.description:
Long-term mortgage rates saw their biggest weekly decline since early March this week, according to the latest Freddie Mac Primary Mortgage Survey.

30-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 6.67% as of July 3, down from 6.77% last week and 6.95% in the year-ago week.

15-year fixed-rate mortgages averaged 5.80%, compared to 5.89% the prior week and 6.25% a year ago.

"The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage decreased for the fifth consecutive week. This is the largest weekly decline since early March," said Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's chief economist.

"Declining mortgage rates are encouraging and, while overall affordability challenges remain, we are seeing more sellers enter the market giving prospective buyers an advantage," added Khater.

Here is a look at the week's detailed survey results:

data.title: Market Voices: June jobs report, Ford CEO warns on AI, UPS buyouts
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's daily roundup of remarks and statements that could impact markets, sectors or individual stocks.

  • The U.S. Labor Department's monthly jobs report was stronger than expected, undermining chances that the Federal Reserve will move to cut interest rates at its meeting later this month. 

According to the report, 147,000 nonfarm jobs were added to the economy last month. Analysts had expected 110,000 new jobs. The unemployment rate for June was 4.1%, falling short of the consensus estimate of 4.3%.

"Job gains occurred in state government and health care. The Federal government continued to lose jobs," the department said in its report. "Employment showed little change over the month in other major industries."

  • Ford CEO Jim Farley is the latest chief executive to warn that AI is poised to decimate white-collar jobs in the United States.

“Artificial intelligence is going to replace literally half of all white-collar workers in the U.S.,” Farley said in a recent interview at the Aspen Ideas Festival.  “AI will leave a lot of white-collar people behind.”

  • According to the Teamsters, UPS (NYSE:UPS) is planning to offer cash buyouts to full-time delivery workers, a move that would violate its national contract with the union.

“UPS is trying to weasel its way out of creating good union jobs here in America by dangling insulting buyouts in front of Teamsters drivers. It is an illegal violation of our national contract,” said Teamsters General President Sean O’Brien, in a statement. “UPS is obligated to establish tens of thousands of new full-time jobs under the agreement."

The Teamsters added that under the current contract, UPS is legally committed to creating 22,500 more jobs.

data.title: Aeries Technology reaffirms FY26 outlook with $74M–$80M revenue target as GCC-driven strategies accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aeries Technology (AERT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ajay Khare reported, "For financial year 2025, we guided to $6 million to $7 million in core adjusted EBITDA. I'm pleased to report that we ended the year having achieving $7.4 million beating our guidance." Khare highlighted that fiscal 2025 was a pivotal year, emphasizing a sharpened strategic focus on core business—specifically supporting private equity-backed companies in building and scaling global capability centers (GCCs)—and a deliberate exit from noncore geographies, including the Middle East consulting markets.
  • The CEO underlined, "Our focus on private equity firms and portfolio companies with a presence in North America has created a resilient and focused business for us." He noted that North America now represents over 93.3% of revenue, with year-over-year North America revenue growth of 15%, from $57 million to $65.5 million.
  • Khare shared updates on major client wins and product development: "We began partnering with a leading global cybersecurity software and services provider to establish and scale global capability centers in both India and Mexico," and signed a letter of intent with a SaaS company in sustainability and compliance to establish AI-driven GCCs.
  • The CEO also announced, "Financial year 2025 marked the launch of our new AI-centered global capability center framework and approach" integrating intelligent automation and generative AI agents for client transformation projects.
  • Daniel Webb, CFO, stated, "For FY 2025, we reported $70.2 million in total revenue compared to $72.5 million in FY 2024. This slight decline was anticipated and driven by our exit from the Middle East business. Excluding that impact, our North America revenue grew 15% year-over-year from $57 million to $65.5 million, demonstrating strong momentum in our core market."
  • Webb further noted, "Our financials in fiscal 2025 reflect onetime items that we don't expect to happen in 2026. The majority of our SG&A in 2025 was from onetime items. The Middle East business has now been fully written off. Our restructuring is complete."

Outlook

  • Webb reaffirmed, "FY 2026 outlook. We are reaffirming our guidance. Revenue, $74 million to $80 million; adjusted EBITDA, $6 million to $8 million." He attributed confidence in these projections to strengthening North American market presence, maturation of the GCC model, and growing demand for digital transformation services among private equity-backed companies.
  • Management cited client traction and the addition of a Chief Growth Officer as supporting continued momentum and expansion in the PE network.
  • The company confirmed that, starting FY 2026, core adjusted EBITDA will no longer be reported as a separate metric, with adjusted EBITDA and GAAP measures becoming the primary financial indicators.

Financial Results

  • Webb reported, "Full year FY 2025, total revenue, $70.2 million; gross profit, $16.7 million, margin of 23.8%. Operating loss, negative $28.8 million; adjusted EBITDA, negative $4.7 million; core adjusted EBITDA, positive $7.4 million; net loss negative $21.6 million."
  • The CFO emphasized, "The core adjusted EBITDA reached $7.4 million, an increase of 365% over the $1.6 million reported in the previous year and above the guidance we have provided."
  • Cash at year-end was $2.8 million with $1.1 million in long-term debt, described by Webb as providing "ample flexibility to support our ongoing initiatives."
  • The CFO stated, "Our restructuring is complete. Stock-based compensation is expected to be significantly lower, we believe 2026 is on track to be our best year yet."

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: No Q&A session or analyst questions were included in the transcript.

Sentiment Analysis

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: No analyst questions or comments were provided in the transcript, so analyst sentiment cannot be assessed.
  • Management's tone throughout the prepared remarks conveyed confidence and optimism. Webb used language such as, "We are confident in this outlook and here's why. We're seeing strong traction with clients. Existing clients are deepening their partnerships."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone shifted from transitional and cautionary to assertively optimistic, reflecting completion of restructuring and clearer focus on growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for FY 2026 ($74 million to $80 million in revenue; $6 million to $8 million adjusted EBITDA) remains consistent with the previous quarter's outlook.
  • Strategic focus sharpened further on core North American markets and private equity-backed companies, building on Q3's initial steps.
  • Cost discipline and restructuring, highlighted in Q3 as ongoing, were declared complete in Q4. Stock-based compensation is projected to decrease in 2026.
  • Key metrics showed improvement in core adjusted EBITDA, rising from $1.6 million in the prior year to $7.4 million, and North America revenue up 15% year-over-year.
  • Management sentiment progressed from a focus on transition and cost optimization in Q3 to confidence in growth and operational stability in Q4.
  • No analyst sentiment comparison is possible due to the lack of Q&A in both quarters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Webb acknowledged that "The majority of our SG&A in 2025 was from onetime items. The Middle East business has now been fully written off. Our restructuring is complete."
  • Management indicated that legacy risks related to noncore geographies have been addressed, and ongoing risks will be managed through a leaner cost structure and focus on North American markets.
  • No explicit concerns from analysts were documented in the transcript.

Final Takeaway

With the completion of its restructuring and a strategic focus on North American private equity-backed clients, Aeries Technology enters FY 2026 with renewed confidence, projecting $74 million to $80 million in revenue and $6 million to $8 million in adjusted EBITDA. The company attributes its momentum to the success of its AI-centered GCC framework, deepening client relationships, and a lean operational model, positioning for sustained growth while legacy challenges from noncore geographies are now fully resolved.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: NXPI, NFLX, RBLX, WOLF
data.description:
A handful of companies on Wall Street have seen recent Seeking Alpha analyst activity, including both upgrades and downgrades. NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) has been upgraded from Hold to Buy, with The Alpha Sieve suggesting the worst may be over for the auto semiconductor provider. Roblox Corporation (RBLX) has seen an upgrade from Bearish to Neutral as its daily active user growth impresses analysts. On the downside, Netflix (NFLX) receives a downgrade from Buy to Hold amid valuation concerns, while Wolfspeed (WOLF) gets downgraded to Sell following its bankruptcy filing and potential delisting risk.

Upgrades

  • NXP Semiconductors N.V. (NXPI): Upgrade Hold to Buy by The Alpha Sieve. The analyst cites stabilizing conditions in NXPI’s automotive segment, favorable valuation metrics, and encouraging technical indicators on the stock’s charts.

    “While there are still some clouds hovering over NXPI, we feel the worst is over, and the medium-term outlook feels more comforting, with solid revenue and earnings growth on the cards. NXPI’s favorable valuation quotient within the semiconductor universe is another appealing facet, while the charts too look encouraging. Because of all these factors, we revise our rating on NXPI from a hold to a buy.”

  • Roblox Corporation (RBLX): Upgrade Bearish to Neutral by Gary Alexander. The analyst acknowledges impressive daily active user growth and accelerating bookings trends, despite continued concerns about the company’s premium valuation.

    “While Roblox’s premium valuation issue has gotten worse, stabilizing DAU trends and in particular, accelerating bookings have me more optimistic about the company’s fundamental prospects. I’m upping my rating on Roblox to neutral… In my view, the upside and downside prospects for Roblox are relatively balanced at current share prices.”

Downgrades

  • Netflix, Inc. (NFLX): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Kenio Fontes. Following a 47.8% increase in stock price since the analyst’s last coverage, the current valuation appears to reflect much of Netflix’s future growth potential, reducing the margin of safety.

    “Due to the fact that the market is now pricing in much of Netflix’s growth potential (which was not priced in before) and also attributes significant quality to the business, I believe a downgrade to hold is necessary. It is not yet a sell rating, since even with the asymmetry decreasing and some uncertainties related to execution and competition, Netflix is delivering great results and still has a lot of room to grow.”

  • Wolfspeed, Inc. (WOLF): Downgrade Hold to Sell by Henrik Alex. Despite a surprising 100% rally following the company’s bankruptcy filing, the analyst warns that the valuation analysis provided in court filings doesn’t support the current share price, while highlighting the increased risk of near-term delisting.

    “Using PWP’s valuation analysis and applying my estimated fully diluted recovery of 3.33% for current equity holders, the implied value of the company’s common shares calculates to a range of $0.18 to $0.29, substantially below Tuesday’s closing price of $0.79… While Wolfspeed likely to emerge from bankruptcy at a much more moderate valuation, investors should consider disposing of existing positions and wait for the new equity to commence trading.”

data.title: Vietnam trade deal likely to shift higher footwear costs to consumers
data.description:
As the dust settles on the new U.S./Vietnam trade deal, Wall Street analysts weigh in on how the agreement will impact the footwear market, one of the most reliant on low-cost overseas labor.

After a knee-jerk reaction lower on Wednesday, shares within the sector made a measured rebound as market players digested the implications on earnings of a 20% import tariff. While the deal removes the uncertainty that was hanging over names like Nike (NYSE:NKE), Skechers (SKX), and Deckers (DECK), the increased cost burden – albeit less than expected – will almost certainly translate into higher prices for consumers.

Evercore ISI analyst Michael Binetti thinks the original 10% Vietnam tariff was “manageable” for manufacturers, “but it’s unlikely that any retailer could completely cover 20% (in addition to the 30% pressure from China) and will have to pass [some of the cost] onto consumers.”

And while the 40% transshipping tariff is aimed at discouraging Chinese manufacturers from skirting U.S. tariffs by moving products through Southeast Asia, it also discourages retailers from moving production out of China.

“Why bother moving from 30% in China to ~25% net tariff in Vietnam?” Binetti asks.

Additionally, if one assumes a trade deal with China will be comparable to Vietnam, a narrower gap between China and Vietnam may encourage some companies that migrated out of China to Vietnam to pivot back to China as Vietnam has higher initial costs and longer lead times, BTIG’s Janine Stichter argues.

For investors, how effectively footwear companies can absorb higher costs is key. A closer look at sourcing shows On Holdings (NYSE:ONON), Deckers (DECK), and Allbirds (BIRD) the most dependent on Vietnam for manufacturing, with 87%, 75%, and 100% of all merchandise made in the Southeast Asian country, respectively. By incorporating the average tariff rate with the percentage of sales in the U.S., Stichter calculates the effective tariff on each, with On Holdings (NYSE:ONON) at 10% from 6%, previously, Deckers (DECK) at 12% from 8%, and Allbirds (BIRD) doubling to 16%.

Counterintuitive to this argument of effective tax rate exposure, Evercore’s Binetti thinks On Holdings (NYSE:ONON) is actually better positioned versus rivals despite a high level of sourcing out of Vietnam. Binetti argues that On (ONON) managed the entire first round of tariffs with just a surgical price increase.

“Pricing power wins, and ONON clearly has the strongest pricing power, the most visibility on where its whole cost base is headed and likely has more flexibility to go back to factories for some contribution,” something On Holdings (ONON) did not do during the initial round of tariff mitigation efforts.

Regardless of which company has the most pricing power, footwear has frequently been a “wallet share loser” during past periods of economic uncertainty, making positive earnings revisions much tougher across the footwear space.
